meaning: n.发烧,发热,热病,狂热,高度兴奋v.(使)发烧,(使)患热病,(使)狂热
examples: He had an earache and a fever.
My Uncle Jim had a high fever.
The fever has passed its crisis.
The child, whose parents are poultry traders, developed a fever, sore throat and headache on Thursday.
Meanwhile, I was experiencing an existential crisis, which involved one thing: Saturday Night Fever.
